scope: G/H group round 2 tactical postmortem only; no score prediction, WDL probability or betting advice.

input_status.source_mode: local postmortem JSON and group reviews prioritized, supplemented by pre-match revisions, first-round reviews and public match reports
input_status.player_state_update_note: Main thread/data closure reports all eight player_state, roster and member-table files received second-round internal rating records, with complete official minutes and same-source external ratings still limited.

public match reports listed in source_log

factor_scale.magnitude: postmatch model adjustment on -1.0 to +1.0 scale; positive usually increases the named factor, risk factors use increase_risk/decrease_risk
factor_scale.confidence: high when result and repeated tactical evidence align; medium when based on one match with incomplete lineup/minute detail; low when role-specific detail is missing

matches.result_context: Belgium again had territorial and shot-volume pressure but did not convert. Iran survived through a compact block, Beiranvand saves and late Belgian red-card context; Taremi also had an offside goal threat and Belgium needed Courtois saves.

formation_and_structure.belgium: Nominal 4-2-3-1/4-3-3 reference remains usable, but possession dominance lacked box occupation and clean final action.
formation_and_structure.iran: 4-2-3-1/4-4-2 low-to-mid block reference remains usable; Taremi outlet and keeper performance were central to game survival.

belgium.tactical_summary: Belgium can still pin opponents back through De Bruyne/Doku corridors, but two group matches show process volume without reliable penalty-box conversion. The red card to Nathan Ngoy and repeated transition concessions keep defensive-line and coach risk elevated.

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Belgium applied territorial pressure but the 10-man phase reduced stable high pressing control.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Do not convert possession pressure into a large pressing upgrade; cap until full pass/press data arrive.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Belgium still reached advanced zones and produced shot volume across two matches.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Use as progression stability, not as finishing quality.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Wide and final-third entries did not create enough clean conversions against Iran's set block.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Downgrade direct transition payoff despite Doku/De Bruyne reputation.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Iran generated Taremi/offside danger and Belgium needed Courtois saves; Ngoy red-card foul came in a recovery context.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Keep separate from generic defense score; this is rest-defense fragility.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Doku and Belgian width still advanced play, but Iran protected central box and forced low-efficiency end product.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Cap wide advantage unless paired with confirmed box target role.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Belgium's midfield possession edge remained visible, led by De Bruyne/Tielemans profile.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Central control should not double count shot volume.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: No decisive Belgian set-piece output in the scoreless match.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Hold pre-match set-piece rating; no postmatch upgrade.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Belgium's recovery defense and red-card event left Iran transition routes alive.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Use as a late-game/game-state risk flag.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Belgium failed to break Iran's block and did not solve attacking balance before the red-card phase.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Challenge Rudi Garcia's attacking adjustment value in model review.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Two games now show Belgian pressure without self-scored goal conversion noted by public reports.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Increase draw/low-conversion scenario in future model features without outputting probabilities here.

iran.tactical_summary: Iran's low/mid block and goalkeeper performance turned Belgium's process edge into a survivable draw. The attacking threat was intermittent but real through Taremi and direct outlets.

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Iran did not need sustained high press; value came from compact resistance.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Model as block strength rather than pressing uplift.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Iran escaped enough pressure to create Taremi threat and force Courtois involvement.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Small upgrade only; complete pass data missing.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Taremi's offside goal threat and Belgian recovery fouls showed outlet danger.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Useful against high-possession teams; do not duplicate Taremi player-state boosts.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Iran absorbed Belgium's wide attacks and survived large territorial pressure.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Credit compactness, but note Beiranvand saves may not repeat at same rate.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Iran mostly defended wide zones rather than creating wide superiority.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Do not penalize too heavily because wide defending was functional.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Belgium held more central possession; Iran's central value was disruption not control.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Separate central control from defensive compactness.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Iran's set-piece/body profile remains relevant, but no decisive event confirmed.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Leave near baseline until event map arrives.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: A disciplined block and goalkeeper saves kept Belgium scoreless.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Cap because goalkeeper overperformance can mask line vulnerability.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Ghalenoei's side preserved structure under heavy pressure and against late game-state changes.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Small coaching credit; detailed substitution timeline missing.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Iran accepted long defensive phases and used isolated transition moments.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Encode as low-tempo/draw-protection tactical state, not as a result prediction.

matches.result_context: New Zealand led through a Finn Surman header, but Egypt's second-half pressure changed the match: Mostafa Ziko headed the equalizer, Salah finished the turnaround and Trezeguet added a corner header.

formation_and_structure.new_zealand: Direct 4-4-2/5-4-1 reference stayed visible through aerial target and set-piece routes, but second-half block and lead management failed.
formation_and_structure.egypt: Egypt moved from low-block counter profile into a more assertive second-half pressure and box-arrival structure, with Salah/Ashour/Ziko/Trezeguet providing layered threats.

new_zealand.tactical_summary: New Zealand's aerial/direct route remained productive enough to lead, but the side again struggled to protect a lead and control second-half pressure.

pressing_adjustment.evidence: New Zealand could not disrupt Egypt's second-half rhythm after taking the lead.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Do not rate them as a sustained pressing team.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: After Egypt increased pressure, New Zealand struggled to keep possession and relieve the block.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Downgrade against teams able to press after halftime.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Direct/aerial route produced the first-half lead through Surman's header.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Keep Wood/direct-play tail but cap after second-half fade.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Egypt repeatedly entered the box after halftime and scored three times through pressure and wide/set-piece routes.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Major postmatch downgrade for lead-protection defense.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Egypt's wide/corner and second-wave routes became more decisive than New Zealand's wide play.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Emam Ashour and Egypt controlled the second-half tempo and central links.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Central control should be reduced when facing technically stronger midfields.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: New Zealand scored via a header route but also conceded Trezeguet's corner header.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Do not blanket-upgrade set pieces; attack and defense split in model.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Second-half defensive spacing collapsed under Egypt pressure.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Game-state management after leading was not solved; public report notes another squandered lead pattern.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Needs substitution timeline from data thread for confirmation.

game_state_tendency.evidence: New Zealand led early but could not handle Egypt's second-half intensity.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Encode strong first-contact threat plus negative late-state resilience.

egypt.tactical_summary: Egypt moved beyond a pure Salah-counter script. The second-half wave involved central orchestration, box arrivals, wide delivery and set-piece threat.

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Egypt's sustained second-half pressure flipped the match after trailing.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Upgrade situational pressing, not necessarily full-match high press.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Ashour helped Egypt keep pressure and connect attacks after halftime.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Small structural upgrade; avoid double counting Ashour form.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Salah and Marmoush-style speed profile remained dangerous; Salah scored during the turnaround.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Maintain but cap if already reflected in Salah player-state.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Egypt conceded to an aerial/direct route but controlled much of the second half.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Keep direct-ball vulnerability, but do not over-penalize after strong response.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Egypt's second-half pressure and corner delivery produced decisive box contacts.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Use as chance-creation route, not just winger ability.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Ashour orchestrated play effectively in public reports and Egypt controlled the second-half rhythm.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Cap against elite midfields; strong here versus New Zealand.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Trezeguet sealed the match with a free header from a corner.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Avoid double counting with goal events; use as defensive-opponent vulnerability signal.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: The early header conceded shows aerial vulnerability, but Egypt's structure improved after halftime.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Keep near neutral with set-piece defense watch.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Hossam Hassan's halftime/second-half adjustment produced pressure, equalizer, lead and set-piece control.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Detailed substitutions missing; treat as coaching game-state credit.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Egypt dominated after halftime and overturned a deficit.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Raise late pressure and comeback-state resilience.

matches.result_context: Spain corrected the Cape Verde stalemate with a fast-starting, aggressive 4-0: Yamal scored early, Oyarzabal added two before the first drinks break, and a second-half own goal followed a corner sequence.

formation_and_structure.spain: Guardian live listed Spain in a 4-2-3-1. Yamal's start, Oyarzabal's penalty-box occupation, Rodri control and early rotation made the attack more vertical than round one.
formation_and_structure.saudi_arabia: Guardian live listed Saudi Arabia in a 5-3-2. The block was overwhelmed before it could settle, and second-half changes arrived after the match state had already tilted.

spain.tactical_summary: Spain restored identity through tempo, width and early box occupation. The key tactical correction from round one was turning possession into early penalty-box actions, then rotating after control was secured.

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Spain played with purposeful aggression and kept Saudi Arabia pinned early.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Raise high-press/control factor when Yamal/Olmo/Baena roles are active.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Rodri completed heavy midfield volume and Spain controlled more than 70% possession in reports.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Strong structural input; avoid double counting possession share.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Spain attacked faster than against Cape Verde, with Yamal and early box runners accelerating attacks.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: This is tempo upgrade, not pure counterattack.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Saudi Arabia produced little stable threat while Spain controlled territory.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Keep small because stronger transition teams can still target fullback space.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Yamal's first start and goal changed Spain's right-side threat; Cucurella/Olmo back-post sequence also mattered.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: High double-counting risk with Yamal player-state; cap individual plus tactical combined uplift.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Rodri, Baena and Olmo helped Spain play through Saudi Arabia's 5-3-2 before the block settled.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Use as central dominance, not as independent finishing boost.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: The fourth came from a corner/back-post sequence ending in an own goal.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Only small set-piece upgrade; open-play superiority was main driver.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Spain's counterpress and control reduced Saudi transition access.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Do not erase fullback-space risk against Uruguay-level runners.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: De la Fuente could remove Yamal and Oyarzabal at halftime and manage load from a position of control.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Credit selection correction and game-state rotation.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Three goals before the first drinks break, then controlled second-half rotation.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Encode a stronger early-start state after round-one frustration.

saudi_arabia.tactical_summary: Saudi Arabia's 5-3-2 did not protect the first 25 minutes. Once Spain scored early, the block lost compactness and the game became damage limitation.

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Saudi Arabia could not press Spain's build-up and spent the early match defending deep.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Downgrade pressing only against elite build-up teams.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Saudi Arabia failed to sustain exits from Spain's early territorial control.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Major downgrade versus high press; do not extrapolate fully to weaker opponents.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Saudi Arabia could not turn Al-Dawsari/Al-Buraikan outlets into meaningful transition threat.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Keep some baseline transition speed, but lower against Spain-style counterpress.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: The 5-3-2 was pulled apart by early width, box entries and second balls.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: High risk when conceding first and forced to open structure.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Yamal and Spain's wide/back-post routes overwhelmed Saudi wide defense.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Treat as wide-defense penalty more than attacking-wide penalty.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Rodri-led Spain controlled midfield and prevented Saudi Arabia from establishing rhythm.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: A corner sequence contributed to the fourth goal via deflection/own goal.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Mark defensive set-piece vulnerability, not attacking set-piece weakness.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Early goals and loose box defending showed poor line/box coordination.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Large downgrade, but opponent quality caveat applies.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Donis's halftime changes came after the game state was already heavily damaged.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Use as response-timing risk; complete substitution list needed.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Saudi Arabia trailed heavily before the first drinks break and spent the rest in containment.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Increase vulnerability to early favorite pressure.

matches.result_context: Cape Verde led through Kevin Pina's free-kick. Uruguay replied before halftime through Maxi Araujo and Agustin Canobbio, but Cape Verde equalized through Helio Varela after a defensive error and survived late pressure.

formation_and_structure.uruguay: Bielsa's high-pressure, wide and vertical structure generated volume and comeback pressure, but defensive errors and game-state disorder undermined control.
formation_and_structure.cape_verde: Cape Verde kept the same low-block/counter and set-piece identity that frustrated Spain, adding more attacking bravery and composure.

uruguay.tactical_summary: Uruguay's pressure and shot volume were real, but Bielsa-ball is currently too open at both ends. Defensive errors and poor control after taking the lead are now repeated tournament risks.

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Uruguay pushed for a winner and generated sustained late pressure.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Keep pressing power, but link to fatigue and defensive exposure.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Uruguay recovered from an early deficit and created enough volume to lead before halftime.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Use moderate upgrade; disorganization limits full control score.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Araujo and Canobbio goals before halftime came from Uruguay's vertical/wide pressure profile.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Avoid double counting with individual goal events.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Cape Verde equalized after a defensive error and repeatedly found upset-path moments despite lower volume.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Valverde/Ugarte crossing and wide service were involved in Uruguay's first-half response according to public reports.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Use as chance route, but not a finishing guarantee.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Uruguay had pressure and territory, but Bielsa admitted disorganization and Cape Verde disrupted rhythm.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Central control should be smaller than pre-match model expected.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Uruguay conceded Kevin Pina's free-kick and did not turn late pressure into a winner.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Downgrade defensive set-piece handling; attack set-pieces not fully assessed.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Two goals conceded, including an avoidable second-half error, after also drawing Saudi Arabia in round one.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: This is a core postmatch parameter correction.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Uruguay pressure did not translate into controlled late-game solutions; public reports frame Bielsa under pressure.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Use as game-state management risk until substitution data arrives.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Uruguay can flip games with pressure but also leaves chaotic late and defensive-error tails.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Increase volatility, not just attack rating.

cape_verde.tactical_summary: Cape Verde repeated their Spain performance with more attacking edge: set-piece quality, composure after conceding twice, and late-block defending now look like stable traits rather than noise.

pressing_adjustment.evidence: Cape Verde mainly relied on block discipline, with selective pressure and counter moments.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Do not model as high press.

build_up_resistance_adjustment.evidence: Cape Verde stayed composed enough to equalize after going behind.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Small upgrade; still not a possession-control side.

transition_attack_adjustment.evidence: Varela's equalizer and repeated danger moments came from exploiting Uruguay's defensive disorder.

transition_defense_risk.evidence: Cape Verde conceded twice before halftime but survived sustained late pressure.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Split first-half vulnerability from late-block resilience.

wide_channel_advantage.evidence: Cape Verde's attacking threat was more set-piece/error/counter based than sustained wide dominance.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Keep near neutral unless winger roles confirmed.

central_control_advantage.evidence: Uruguay had more pressure and central territory, but Cape Verde disrupted enough to avoid collapse.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Do not over-penalize due to effective defensive block.

set_piece_advantage.evidence: Kevin Pina scored a powerful free-kick; set pieces are now a confirmed upset route.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Avoid double counting Pina's goal in player_state; keep as team set-piece route.

defensive_line_risk.evidence: Despite conceding two, Cape Verde blocked late Uruguay pressure and repeated strong low-block survival from Spain match.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Risk reduction applies to late low-block state, not open first half.

coach_substitution_risk.evidence: Cape Verde maintained belief and shape after conceding twice and through late pressure.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Credit game-state management; detailed substitution sequence missing.

game_state_tendency.evidence: Draws against Spain and Uruguay show low-block resilience plus set-piece/counter scoring path.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Model as repeatable upset/draw-state trait, not one-off noise.

cross_match_tactical_lessons: G/H favorites with possession volume need separate conversion and rest-defense parameters; Belgium and Uruguay created pressure without control.
Low-block underdogs with verified set-piece or goalkeeper routes should not be treated as passive only: Iran and Cape Verde created real result-protection mechanisms.
Spain's 4-0 is a tactical correction from round one: early width, box occupation and halftime load management repaired the low-block problem against a weaker defensive structure.
Egypt's 3-1 shows second-half pressure and central orchestration can be added to the prior Salah-only transition profile.
New Zealand's direct/aerial threat remains real, but lead management and second-half resistance require downgrade.
double_counting_warnings: Spain wide_channel_advantage overlaps with Lamine Yamal player-state and goal event; cap the combined uplift.
Egypt transition_attack_adjustment overlaps with Salah and Trezeguet/Ziko goal events; use as route confirmation, not extra finishing form.
Cape Verde set_piece_advantage overlaps with Kevin Pina's free-kick goal; keep as team route and do not double-add to player form.
Belgium process_without_conversion should not be counted both as attack downgrade and draw-tail increase without a cap.
Uruguay defensive_line_risk and transition_defense_risk describe overlapping Bielsa rest-defense exposure; aggregate cautiously.
data_gaps: Local round2 postmatch JSON and Markdown are now available but remain partial_source_limited.
Official starting XIs, formations, substitution minutes, cards and complete event feeds still need full data-collector extraction.
Eight roster/player_state/member-table files have second-round internal rating records, but full official per-player minutes and same-source external ratings remain incomplete.
Same-source xG, shot map, possession, pass network and set-piece event maps are missing or only partially available.
Some public sources disagree on exact Uruguay/Cape Verde second-goal error mechanics; treat as generic defensive-error evidence until official event log is imported.
Coach substitution claims are medium confidence because detailed substitution timelines were not locally available.

source_log.source: Guardian match report: Spain 4-0 Saudi Arabia
Guardian match report: Uruguay 2-2 Cape Verde
Local G/H first-round reviews and round2 quant revisions
